story.title: Import existing Frigate config YAML (paste or file) and redraw zones/masks on the canvas
story.as_a: Frigate user with an existing hand-written config.yml
story.i_want: to paste my config or a camera snippet and see my zones and masks drawn over my camera frame
story.so_that: I can visually verify and refine polygons I originally calculated by hand, without retyping coordinates

acceptance_criteria: Given a full Frigate config.yml pasted into the import panel, when Import is clicked, then all zones (cameras.<cam>.zones.<name>.coordinates), motion masks (cameras.<cam>.motion.mask and top-level motion.mask), and object filter masks (objects.filters.<type>.mask at either level) are extracted and rendered as closed polygons with correct names, types, and object types
Given a bare snippet (zones:/motion: at top level, as this app exports), when imported, then polygons import identically to the full-config case

Given coordinates in normalized form ('0.448,0.859,...'), when imported, then points map 1:1 to normalized state; given legacy pixel-form coordinates (values > 1, e.g. '860,173,...'), when imported with an image loaded, then values are divided by image dimensions and a banner notes the pixel→normalized conversion

Given a config containing multiple cameras, when imported before multi-camera (spec 08) ships, then a camera picker lists detected camera names and the user imports one camera's polygons into the workspace

Given invalid YAML or YAML with no recognizable zones/masks, when Import is clicked, then an inline error states what was looked for and nothing in the workspace changes (import is all-or-nothing per attempt)

Given imported polygons, when the user edits, deletes, or exports them, then they behave identically to hand-drawn polygons (ids assigned, validation applied with warn-only for non-conforming legacy names)
constraints: Parse with js-yaml, vendored minified inside the HTML file under a clearly-commented section with version and source URL (locked decision D1); YAML generation remains hand-rolled
Read-only import per master spec: no round-trip writing back to original file structure; imported data becomes ordinary workspace polygons
Importer namespace: Importer.parse(text) → { cameras: [{name, zones:[], motionMasks:[], objectMasks:[]}], warnings: [] } — pure, no Store access; a separate apply step commits to Store
Coordinate heuristic: if any value in a polygon > 1.001, treat the whole polygon as pixel-space (requires loaded image; otherwise block that polygon's import with a warning to upload the image first)
Import UI: collapsible 'Import YAML' card in the left column with textarea + file picker (accept .yml,.yaml) + Import button
Existing-name collisions on import get a numeric suffix (driveway → driveway_2) with a warning listing renames
edge_cases: Frigate also accepts list-of-pairs coordinate form ('- 0.448,0.859' per line list) — support both string and list forms
YAML anchors/aliases and comments — handled by js-yaml transparently
Empty mask string or coordinates with odd value count — skip that polygon, add to warnings, import the rest (per-polygon skip is allowed; all-or-nothing applies only to hard parse failures)
Coordinates out of 0–1 range after conversion — import anyway, range warning fires via existing Validate rule
Duplicate zone names within the same imported camera — suffix and warn
Very large config (50+ cameras) — picker renders as scrollable list; parse stays synchronous (js-yaml on <1MB text is milliseconds)
risks: Medium: real-world configs vary widely (includes, env substitutions like {FRIGATE_RTSP_PASSWORD}) — js-yaml parses them as plain strings, which is fine since we only read polygon paths
Low: vendored js-yaml adds ~40KB to the file — accepted in locked decision D1
Medium: pixel-vs-normalized heuristic misfires on a legitimate normalized value of exactly 1.0 — threshold 1.001 plus whole-polygon (not per-value) classification mitigates

open_questions.question: When importing, should existing workspace polygons be kept or replaced?

options.label: Merge with rename-on-collision (Recommended)
options.info: Non-destructive; undo toast not needed; collisions auto-suffixed and reported.

options.label: Replace workspace
options.info: Cleaner result but destructive; would require undo snapshot.

tasks.description: Vendor minified js-yaml into the HTML with provenance comment; smoke-test load() from file://

Implement Importer.parse: walk full-config and snippet shapes, extract zones/motion masks/object masks per camera, parse both string and list coordinate forms, classify pixel vs normalized, produce warnings
Build import UI (collapsible card, textarea, .yml file picker, camera picker when >1 camera, warnings list) and Store.applyImport with collision suffixing
Build importer test corpus: app's own export, full Frigate sample config from docs, pixel-coordinate legacy config, list-form coordinates, anchored YAML, garbage input
QA: run corpus through UI, verify round-trip (import own export → re-export → diff equals input modulo key order), verify all-or-nothing on parse failure

docs_md:
## YAML Import
Paste an existing config.yml (or any snippet containing zones/motion/objects blocks) or pick a .yml file. Detected polygons are drawn on the canvas as normal editable items. Pixel-space legacy coordinates are auto-converted using the loaded image's dimensions. Multi-camera configs prompt for which camera to import (until the multi-camera workspace ships). Import never writes back to your original file.

stub_suggestions.purpose: Pure parse layer, fully unit-testable from console

metrics: 100% of QA corpus files import with expected polygon counts, names, and types
Round-trip fidelity: importing the app's own export then re-exporting produces semantically identical YAML (same polygons, coords within 0.001)
Hard parse failure changes 0 workspace state fields (deep-equal before/after)

decision_log.rationale: A real YAML parser is non-negotiable for user-authored configs (anchors, comments, flow style); vendoring js-yaml preserves the single-file air-gap constraint. Pure Importer.parse separated from Store.applyImport keeps parsing unit-testable and makes the multi-camera upgrade (spec 08) a change to apply, not parse.
alternatives_considered: Rejected: hand-rolled regex/indent parser — silently corrupts edge-case configs; worst possible failure mode for a tool whose output goes into a security system
Rejected: CDN-loaded js-yaml — violates air-gap/no-network constraint
Rejected: importing camera ffmpeg/detect settings too — master spec scopes v0.2 import to polygons; camera import arrives naturally with spec 08
tradeoffs: Single-camera apply until spec 08; multi-camera configs need per-camera import passes
decision_log.confidence_rationale: Parser is solid; the long tail of real-world config shapes is the residual risk, mitigated by the QA corpus and per-polygon skip-with-warning behavior.

defaults_applied: Assuming js-yaml 4.x as the vendored parser
Assuming pixel-space classification threshold of 1.001 applied per-polygon
Assuming Frigate v0.13+ config key shapes; older review_/track formats not specifically handled (version presets are a v0.5 concern)
